prompt_text: Give me a list of "superfoods" I should add to my diet that are available at a discount dollar store.
response_text:
# Budget-Friendly Superfoods at Dollar Stores

**Canned & Shelf-Stable:**
- Canned beans (black, pinto, chickpeas) - protein and fiber
- Canned fish (tuna, salmon) - omega-3s and protein
- Canned vegetables - still nutritious, often cheaper than fresh
- Oats - affordable whole grain staple
- Peanut butter - protein and healthy fats
- Nuts and seeds (if available) - micronutrients

**Produce (when in season/available):**
- Bananas - potassium, usually very cheap
- Eggs - complete protein
- Frozen vegetables - often cheaper than fresh, equally nutritious
- Carrots - affordable, nutrient-dense root vegetable

**Other Options:**
- Whole wheat bread
- Greek yogurt (sometimes discounted)
- Dried beans and lentils

**A note:** Dollar stores vary widely in selection and quality. Stick with items in their original packaging when possible, and check expiration dates. The "superfood" concept is somewhat marketing-driven—consistency with affordable basics (beans, eggs, oats, frozen vegetables) matters more than chasing trendy items.

The best diet is one you can actually afford and maintain long-term.

extracted.summary: The response provides a list of budget-friendly superfoods available at dollar stores, including canned goods, produce, and other options.
caveats_and_disclaimers: Dollar stores vary widely in selection and quality. Stick with items in their original packaging when possible, and check expiration dates.
